evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent v2 API surface: /v1/oauth/token, /checkout/v2/pay, /checkout/v2/order/{id}/status, O-Bearer header, client_credentials grant. X-VERIFY SHA256(base64+path+salt)###index matches documented pattern. No clear deprecated v1 trap; consistent and version-claimed (v2 post-2024).
evidence.executability: Python typo `cancelled = True` in JSX cleanup (should be `true`). Pydantic v1 BaseSettings import (`from pydantic import BaseSettings`) deprecated in v2 — needs pydantic-settings. `str | None` requires Python 3.10+. Multiple code blocks would write to same main.py (partial). Webhook hash comparison may miss SHA256 hex case. Several fixes needed.
evidence.completeness: (a) credential acquisition described (PhonePe Business Dashboard, Developer Settings); (b) install commands present as requirements.txt not bash pip install; (c) env vars referenced via Settings class but no explicit .env template block with KEY=VALUE; (d) backend code present though split into 'partial' fragments; (e) frontend code present; (f) test command absent — pytest example shown but no test invocation command. Env var names listed only via Pydantic field names, not extractable .env template.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are bare bracketed numbers [14][22][7] etc. with no URLs to developer.phonepe.com. No canonical official docs URL anywhere in the document. Cannot verify any citation source.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true: applied long-tail handling. Internal consistency strong, version claimed (v2), so correctness scored 4. Citations as numeric markers with no URLs hurts anchoring badly. Completeness reduced for split 'partial' code blocks that a deterministic extractor would concatenate incorrectly, missing explicit .env template, and missing test command in bash block.
